task: Fixed patching strategy in context-cut handover tests by promoting filesystem paths to module-level constants in stop.py, enabling proper mock.patch targeting and achieving 15/15 test passes.
discussion_summary: The test suite was failing because `ExitStack` import was wrong and path variables were local to functions, making them impossible to patch. Moved `HANDOVER_PATH` and `HANDOVER_BACKUP_PATH` to module level in stop.py (matching the pattern already used in lifecycle.py), then updated test patches to target `askr.session.lifecycle._infer_direction` and other imports at their source module. All 15 tests now pass. Committed changes to main.

accomplishments.what: Promoted filesystem path constants to module level in askr/hooks/stop.py
Fixed import statement (ExitStack from contextlib, not unittest.mock)
Updated test patches to target imports at source module (askr.session.lifecycle)
Committed and pushed changes to main branch

next_actions.action: Verify pre_compact.py hook fires before compaction and blocks auto-compaction when context limit is triggered
next_actions.why: This is the next open goal — need to ensure the pre-compaction hook integration works end-to-end

next_actions.action: Run end-to-end test: trigger 75% context limit and verify autonomous task-cut session launches without approval gate
next_actions.why: Second open goal — validate the full context-cut scenario works in practice, not just unit tests

next_actions.action: Review handover document structure generated by stop.py to ensure it contains all required fields for autonomous agent continuation
next_actions.why: Ensure the JSON handover format is complete and parseable by next session

decisions.decision: Moved path constants to module level in stop.py rather than using real filesystem save/restore in tests
decisions.reason: Matches existing pattern in lifecycle.py; cleaner and more testable than filesystem mocking

decisions.decision: Patched imports at source module (askr.session.lifecycle) rather than at call site
decisions.reason: Imports happen at function definition time; patching at source ensures mocks are in place before function executes

failed_approaches.approach: Patching paths as local variables inside the function using mock.patch context managers
failed_approaches.reason: Local variables cannot be patched; they exist only at runtime inside the function scope

failed_approaches.approach: Using real filesystem paths with save/restore in tests
failed_approaches.reason: Fragile and slow; module-level constants with patching is cleaner

relational_files.why: stop.py imports _infer_direction and other functions from lifecycle; tests patch these at source
Tests the stop.py module's context-cut handover logic
